title: Rename long cirrus job names
body:
Basically, add a 'name' tag (remote, local) to each incantation
of TEST_REMOTE_CLIENT: true/false

I have no idea if this will work or what results it'll produce.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Looks promising so far:
```diff
- testing TEST_REMOTE_CLIENT:true image:fedora-31-libpod-594030756...
+ testing image:fedora-31-libpod-5940307564953600 name:remote
```
I had hoped it would chop off the `name:`, but that's only 5 characters, and overall this gives us 11 characters max vs 24. 

Cirrus is throttling me because of too-quickly-repeated jobs; I'll wait a while before retrying.
@cevich PTAL. I tried everything I could think of; there doesn't seem to be a way to get rid of the `-libpod-123456789` suffix. IMHO this is still good enough as it is.

That's the VM image name, which is handled a bit differently as it goes directly into the 'gce_instance' map.  Since originally crafted, Cirrus enabled support for using env. vars to indirectly hold the image names.  It will take a bit of re implementing to put this in place, but the result is the env. var name will be shown instead of the VM image name.  IIRC, we're using this in another repo. currently, I'll post a link once I can find it...

...found it in [containers/storage configuration](https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/master/.cirrus.yml#L54-L96).  Example of what it looks like (using Cirrus WebUI for convenience):  https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6574673093722112

Point is, the variable name possibly doesn't need to be so verbose.  For instance `$FEDORA_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME` -> `$FIMG1` and `$PRIOR_FEDORA_CACHE_IMAGE_NAME` -> `$FIMG2` (or whatever is short and makes sense).

Though I'm seeing `image: fully-spelled-out-name-122343455667` appear as well.  Not sure if that affects...where are you trying to use the unique (human readable) task name from?

I'm not sure we're talking about the same thing. For the first part of this PR (`TEST_REMOTE_CLIENT`), a shorter variable name would help, but I think this approach is better. For the second part, I don't think variable names come into play at all. I was just trying to get rid of the useless `libpod-XXXXXXX` garbage that gets displayed on github results. Unfortunately I can't find any way to do so.
Filed https://github.com/cirruslabs/cirrus-ci-docs/issues/589 in hopes of getting a way to display user-provided names
OMG everybody look at how nice and readable those CI job names are!
sweet!
LGTM
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/hold

@cevich are you responsible for podbot? If so can you check if there's some unwelcome interaction between this PR and podbot?

The short version is: this PR took a lot of retries to get to pass. There were two jobs flaking over and over again: fedora-30 remote and local. Eventually, one of those finally passed. When it did, podbot pinged me (twice!) on #podman :
```
13:55:18 <podbot_50205371> edsantiago: Cirrus-CI testing successful for PR 'Rename long cirrus job names': https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5387
13:56:11 <podbot_56650334> edsantiago: Cirrus-CI testing successful for PR 'Rename long cirrus job names': https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5387
```
Two different podbots, almost a minute apart. But it was a lie: my job had not finished. The second job was still running; it finally passed about ten minutes later (and there was no corresponding podbot message on IRC).

More info: doesn't look like my PR's fault. I just saw podbot spit out five identical messages for one unrelated PR:
```
20:48:33 <podbot_65422381> sgrunert: Cirrus-CI testing successful for PR 'Fix podman image sign help output': https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5389
20:48:33 <podbot_49421838> sgrunert: Cirrus-CI testing successful for PR 'Fix podman image sign help output': https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5389
20:48:33 <podbot_59304614> sgrunert: Cirrus-CI testing successful for PR 'Fix podman image sign help output': https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5389
20:48:33 <podbot_56459706> sgrunert: Cirrus-CI testing successful for PR 'Fix podman image sign help output': https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5389
20:48:38 <podbot_53830764> sgrunert: Cirrus-CI testing successful for PR 'Fix podman image sign help output': https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5389
```
The PR in question is green, but I wasn't paying close enough attention to check if it went green before those messages or after.

> When it did, podbot pinged me (twice!) on #podman 

Those notices come from the `success_task` script.  It's possible for there to be multiple executions of the success task at the same time.  I believe this is caused by a combination of multiple side-effects involving dependent tasks.  I debugged it fully once, but couldn't find a simple way to guarantee only once notice is sent per sha.

This is another thing we could move into post-cirrus automation, where we can have more guarantees about the actual completion-state of the overall build.

> doesn't look like my PR's fault.

Not I highly doubt it's anything you did.

Thanks. I've seen some weird podbot messages the past 24 hours, and I concur.

/hold cancel

@edsantiago this is really really fantastic, thank you again for helping with these things.  The name length was a loud complaint I heard frequently during initial implementation, but (at the time) was impossible to fix.

One minor suggestion in addition to my comments above:  Consider if we could just drop the term 'testing' entirely from the displayed names.  Everybody here knows / assumes most/all activities here are about or for "testing".  Might help get the names even shorter :grinning: 

In any case, this is a vast improvement as-is and I think the results speak for themselves.

LGTM as-is after fixing up `contrib/cirrus/cirrus_yaml_test.py`, it's fine if you prefer to do followup PRs for my other suggestions.

> Consider if we could just drop the term 'testing' entirely from the displayed names

I shortened ti to `test`. I'd like to keep _some_ common prefix, because Cirrus/Github sorts results. A common prefix helps cluster everything in `testing_task` together, making it easier IMO for the eye to pick up common success/failure elements.

I would like to explore further renaming of tasks, but believe a followup PR would be better.

Thanks for your feedback! That was helpful; Fixes, cleanup, and rebase coming soon.
okay, follow-up PRs are perfectly fine by me, let's get this in quick so everyone can benefit as-is...
/lgtm
